generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LidarSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor that produces raw point‑cloud data for the 1/10‑scale autonomous vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LidarDataTransmitted
]

instance SpeedSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle‑state sensor providing current speed values"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SpeedDataTransmitted
]

instance PositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle‑state sensor providing position (x,y) coordinates"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle‑state sensor reporting motor status (on/off, RPM)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorStatusDataTransmitted
]

instance SteeringAngleSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor delivering current steering angle of the miniature vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringAngleDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction (Data transmitted over ROS2 → Wi‑Fi UDP)
instance LidarDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LidarSensor
]

instance SpeedDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SpeedSensor
]

instance PositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionSensor
]

instance MotorStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorStatusSensor
]

instance SteeringAngleDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringAngleSensor
]

// C5: Data instances
instance RawPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unprocessed point‑cloud frames received from the LiDAR sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LidarDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleSpeedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current vehicle speed measured by the Speed sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SpeedDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationEnabler
]

instance VehiclePositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current vehicle position (x,y) measured by the Position sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationEnabler
]

instance MotorStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current motor status (on/off, RPM) reported by the MotorStatus sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorStatusDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationEnabler
]

instance SteeringAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current steering angle reported by the SteeringAngle sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringAngleDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Object tracks generated by the Point‑Cloud Clustering & Tracking model"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationEnabler
]

instance DesiredSpeedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Desired speed output from the Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) model"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationEnabler
]

instance BrakeCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Brake command (activate/deactivate) issued by the ACC model"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationEnabler
]

// C10: Models / Data processing
instance PointCloudClusteringTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric clustering (DBSCAN) and Kalman‑filter tracking of LiDAR point clouds"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreProcessingEnabler
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral ACC model that selects a lead vehicle and computes desired speed"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationEnabler
]

instance DataTranslationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Serializes streams, aligns message types and performs unit conversions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers (Functional Entities)
instance PreProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity that executes point‑cloud clustering & tracking"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ComputationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity that runs the ACC algorithm using vehicle state and object tracks"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity that performs ROS2 → UDP serialization and unit conversion"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ActuationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity that forwards speed, brake and steering commands to the Physical Twin actuators"
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity that renders real‑time vehicle state and object tracks in the UI"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real‑time UI visualization of vehicle state, projected path and object tracks"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVehicleStateInsight, ObjectTrackVisualizationInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes ACC computation off‑board and returns speed & brake commands"
    DTDFVocab:provides DesiredSpeedCommandAction, BrakeCommandAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Allows operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speed and issue emergency brake"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction, SetDesiredSpeedAction, EmergencyBrakeAction
]

// C17: Insights
instance RealTimeVehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight displaying current speed, position and motor status in the monitoring UI"
]

instance ObjectTrackVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight showing clustered object tracks and predicted trajectories"
]

// C17: Actions
instance DesiredSpeedCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic command generated by ACC to set vehicle speed"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance BrakeCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic brake activation command generated by ACC"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator‑initiated action to enable or disable the ACC algorithm"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator‑initiated action to manually set a target speed"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator‑initiated emergency brake command"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Commands originating from the Digital Twin Entity (e.g., ACC‑derived desired speed, enable/disable ACC, emergency‑brake request) are issued by the Control Panel (User Interface FE) and forwarded through the Data Translation FE, then sent over the same Wi‑Fi UDP link to the Physical Twin Entity. The Actuation Functional Entity on the Physical Twin receives the desired speed command, converts it into motor voltage signals, and drives the vehicle actuators; enable/disable and emergency‑brake commands directly trigger the Actuation FE to stop or modify actuation. Command messages are serialized and unit‑converted by Data Translation, providing basic format validation; the system relies on the ROS 2/DDS infrastructure for reliable delivery, but no explicit authentication or conflict‑resolution mechanisms are described."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The ACC digital twin requires the physical and digital components to share a common notion of time with clock synchronization accuracy of 100 ms, as imposed by hardware limits and safety‑critical vehicular standards. All services (data monitoring, computation offloading, remote management) operate in real‑time; the system must provide low end‑to‑end latency communication between the vehicle and the DT, though the exact latency bound is not quantified in the documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The overall orchestration follows the ISO 23247 reference architecture, mapping each Functional Entity to an individual ROS 2 package; each package contains one or more ROS 2 nodes that implement the FE functionality. Intra‑entity communication uses the ROS 2 publish/subscribe network, while inter‑entity communication is carried over a Wi‑Fi network using UDP at the transport layer. A Data Translation FE serialises all streams, aligns message types and performs unit conversions. Services are composed of SW components (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking, ACC Controlling, Reporting) that are orchestrated by the DT Entity and invoked by the User Interface FE."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is ensured by maintaining real‑time synchronization of all components within a 100 ms bound, as required for safety‑critical vehicular applications. The ACC behavioral model’s output is validated visually on the 3D Visualization UI and quantitatively through the Reporting FE, which logs desired speed versus actual vehicle response. The point‑cloud clustering & tracking model’s accuracy is supported by the Data Translation FE that enforces consistent units and message formats, reducing data‑type errors. Uncertainty sources include sensor noise from LiDAR and vehicle‑state measurements; these are mitigated by the preprocessing FE that filters and clusters raw data before it is used by higher‑level models. Quality assurance is performed by the Data Storage FE that persists raw and processed data for post‑run analysis, and by the Reporting FE that generates reports for developers to assess model performance."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DTS employs a hybrid network architecture: intra‑entity software components communicate via a ROS 2 network (DDS) on each host, while inter‑entity traffic between the Physical Twin, Digital Twin, and User entities uses a Wi‑Fi network with UDP transport. Clock synchronization among devices is required with ≤ 100 ms accuracy to support safety‑critical off‑loading. The network is designed for low end‑to‑end latency to meet real‑time monitoring and remote‑management requirements. No explicit bandwidth figures are given; reliability is based on ROS 2 QoS settings and UDP, and security protocols (e.g., encryption or authentication) are not detailed in the documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DTS is deployed across both edge (on‑board vehicle hardware) and external compute platforms. The Data Pre‑Processing FE (LiDAR clustering & tracking) and the Actuation FE reside on the vehicle (edge) as ROS 2 packages, providing immediate access to sensors and actuators. Higher‑level services—Visualization FE, Data Collection FE, Reporting FE, ACC Controlling FE, and the User Interface FE—run on an external host (e.g., cloud or edge server) that offers greater processing capacity. Collected vehicle state and object tracks are persisted in an external SQL database (Data Storage). The architecture leverages modular ROS 2 nodes, enabling scalability by adding or replicating services, and supports deployment models ranging from single‑machine to distributed cloud‑edge configurations. Availability is addressed by separating critical actuation (on‑vehicle) from non‑critical analytics (off‑board), though explicit redundancy or failover mechanisms are not described."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "External integration is realised through ROS 2 middleware for internal data exchange and UDP over Wi‑Fi for communication between the physical twin and the DT. The Data Translation FE may be implemented with OPC UA (IEC 62541) to provide standardized data‑exchange semantics. The system therefore inter‑operates with external devices and enterprise tools via OPC UA‑compatible interfaces and standard network protocols (ROS 2, UDP)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The automotive digital twin is implemented in compliance with ISO 23247 (Digital Twin Manufacturing Framework), specifically Part 2’s reference architecture, employing the entity‑based model (Physical Twin Entity, Digital Twin Entity, User Entity) and mapping each Functional Entity to a ROS 2 package. The design references IEC 62541 (OPC UA) for the Data Translation functional entity and adheres to a ≤ 100 ms clock‑synchronisation requirement for safety‑critical vehicular use. Safety considerations are linked to ISO 26262, and the architecture is aligned with AUTOSAR principles to enable hardware‑agnostic component deployment. The prototype is released as open‑source code via the McMaster Centre for Software Certification, supporting interoperability with existing automotive standards."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) follows the ACC control strategy defined in ISO 15622 (performance class I, no road‑curvature capability) as required by requirement R6, thereby grounding the safety‑critical control loop in an established automotive functional‑safety specification. The case‑study discussion (Document 2) stresses that safety must be promoted to a first‑class citizen in automotive DT standards and recommends alignment with ISO 26262 functional‑safety requirements for any safety‑critical DT functions. Security concerns are acknowledged in the same study, which calls for dedicated security research and highlights the relevance of blockchain‑based e/e‑traceability for data integrity as described by Heber et al. (Document 3). No specific cybersecurity protocols, access‑control mechanisms, or authentication schemes are detailed in the provided documents."
]
